appointment-type.title: --WELSH What would you like to discuss?

options.hint: --WELSH Explains the options for taking money from your defined contribution pension.
--WELSH Divorce and dissolution can be complicated and stressful. Getting impartial guidance from us can make managing your pension options easier.
--WELSH Speak to a pensions specialist if you are self-employed and need help understanding your pension options.
--WELSH For people who have lost pension money due to a scam or fraud and need support.

radio-button.error: --WELSH Select what type of appointment you would like.

sections.content: --WELSH A free Pension Wise appointment explains the options for taking money from your defined contribution pension. It’s impartial and backed by government.
--WELSH You’ll find out:
items: --WELSH when you can access your pension pots
--WELSH the different ways you can take money from your pension
--WELSH how each option is usually taxed
--WELSH how to spot and avoid scams.

sections.content: --WELSH You can’t book a Pension Wise appointment if:
items: --WELSH you’re under 50
--WELSH you only have a defined benefit pension (final salary or career-average salary) or
--WELSH you only have an overseas pension.

sections.content: --WELSH If you’re self-employed, pension options can feel complicated and hard to compare. This appointment helps you understand how pensions work when you work for yourself, and what your choices are.
--WELSH You’ll find out:
items: --WELSH the types of pensions available to self-employed people
--WELSH how contributions work and what you can afford to save
--WELSH how tax relief is applied and what you could get back
--WELSH what to consider when choosing a pension provider or scheme
--WELSH how to check your state pension forecast
--WELSH how to spot risks and avoid pension scams
--WELSH where to get free, impartial guidance if you need more help

sections.content: --WELSH You **can’t** book a Self-employed appointment if:
items: --WELSH you aren’t self-employed

sections.content:
--WELSH Our divorce and separation section is here to give you all the guidance you need when it comes to managing your finances during a relationship break-up.

It doesn’t matter what your situation is. You may be divorcing or separating; have children or not; own your home or rent; or live in England, Scotland, Wales or Northern Ireland. We have guides that will cover your specific situation.

--WELSH Divorce and dissolution can be complicated and stressful. Getting impartial guidance from us can make managing your pension options easier.

During your appointment, you'll speak with one of our pension specialists who will:
items: --WELSH explain the pension options available during a divorce/dissolution
--WELSH help you understand what to consider and the questions to ask
--WELSH guide you on your next steps, including where to find additional help and how to access regulated financial advice if needed.

sections.content: --WELSH You **can’t** book a divorce and separation appointment if:
items: --WELSH you have agreed a financial settlement in the divorce proceedings

sections.content:
--WELSH There is scams support out there, but it can be overwhelming to navigate when you’ve been scammed. It can also come at a price that people cannot afford.

That’s why we have a specialist free Pension Loss service. Our pension specialists help consumers to make sense of what has happened and all the bewildering acronyms. We discuss the possible redress options and provide helpful resources at no charge. This can be enough to enable a scams victim to approach the right organisations to recover at least some of the money they have lost.
--WELSH You’ll find out:
items: --WELSH what has happened to your pension and what it means for you
--WELSH what common scam-related pension terms and acronyms refer to
--WELSH what options you may have to recover some of the money you’ve lost
--WELSH what to expect next, including possible investigations or follow-up steps

sections.content: --WELSH You **can’t** book a pension loss appointment if:
items: --WELSH you have not been the victim of a suspected or confirmed pension scam

pension-wise-pre-appointment.title: --WELSH What you need for your Pension Wise appointment

sections.content: --WELSH So we can understand your plans for retirement, we’ll usually ask you about your pensions and other finances. You’ll usually benefit more from a Pension Wise appointment if, before your appointment, you:
--WELSH **Ask each of your pension providers:**
items: --WELSH how much your pension is currently worth
--WELSH if your pension has any special features, like a guaranteed pot value or annuity rate.

sections.content: --WELSH **Check your State Pension forecast:**
items: --WELSH [online on GOV.UK](https://www.gov.uk/check-state-pension) or
--WELSH call [0800 731 0175](tel:08007310175) between 8am and 6pm, Monday to Friday.

sections.content: --WELSH **Have details of your other finances, like:**
items: --WELSH savings and investments
--WELSH debts and repayments.

sections.content: --WELSH **Think about your plans for retirement, such as:**
items: --WELSH when you’d like to stop working
--WELSH what type of pension income you’d like.

sections.content: --WELSH On the next screen, you will be prompted to book your appointment date and time, but be mindful of having time to collate the information above to get the most out of your appointment.

self-employed-pre-appointment.title: --WELSH What you need for your self-employed pensions appointment

sections.content: --WELSH You do not need to have everything, but bringing any of the following will help us give you clearer guidance:
items: --WELSH any pension statements you already have
--WELSH details of pension schemes you’ve paid into before
--WELSH information about any savings or investments linked to your retirement
--WELSH your State Pension forecast, if you have one

divorce-pre-appointment.title: --WELSH What you need for your divorce and separation appointment

sections.content: --WELSH So we can understand your plans for retirement, we’ll usually ask you about your pensions and other finances. You’ll usually benefit more from a divorce and separation appointment if, before your appointment, you:
--WELSH Ask each of your pension providers:
items: --WELSH how much your pension is currently worth
--WELSH if your pension has any special features, like a guaranteed pot value or annuity rate.

sections.content: --WELSH Check your State Pension forecast:
items: --WELSH [online on GOV.UK (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gov.uk/check-state-pension)
--WELSH call [0800 731 0175](tel:08007310175) between 8am and 6pm, Monday to Friday.

sections.content: --WELSH Have details of your other finances, like:
items: --WELSH savings and investments
--WELSH debts and repayments.

sections.content: --WELSH Think about your plans for retirement, such as:
items: --WELSH when you’d like to stop working

sections.content: --WELSH On the next screen, you will be prompted to book your appointment date and time, but be mindful of having time to collate the information above to get the most out of your appointment.

pension-loss-pre-appointment.title: --WELSH What you need for your pension loss appointment

sections.content: --WELSH You do not need to have everything listed, but bringing any of the following can help us understand your situation more quickly:
items: --WELSH Any letters, emails or messages you received about the pension investment or transfer
--WELSH Copies of forms you signed, including transfer or investment paperwork
--WELSH Statements from your pension provider showing recent activity or transactions
--WELSH Screenshots of adverts, websites or social media posts linked to the scam
--WELSH The name and contact details of anyone you dealt with
--WELSH Any references, policy numbers or pension scheme details you have
--WELSH Notes about what happened and when, including dates of calls or messages

eligibility-defined-contribution.title: --WELSH Do you have a defined contribution pension?

sections.content: --WELSH This is the type of pension where you or your employer pay into a pot, and its value can go up or down depending on investments. You can use our tool to [find out your pension type (opens in a new tab)](https://tool.moneyhelper.org.uk/en/pension-type/question-1).

radio-button.label: --WELSH Do you have a defined contribution pension?

options.text: --WELSH No, I have a different type of pension

radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether you have a defined contribution pension.

radio-button.label: --WELSH Are you aged 50 or over?
radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether you are over 50 years old

eligibility-uk-pensions.title: --WELSH Do you have any pensions based in the UK?

sections.content: --WELSH You can book an appointment if you have at least one UK-based pension. For example, a pension you built up while working or living in the UK.

radio-button.label: --WELSH Do you have any pensions based in the UK?
radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether you only have an overseas pension

eligibility-age-exceptions.title: --WELSH Do any of the following apply to you?

radio-button.label: --WELSH Do any of the following apply to you?

options.text: --WELSH You have inherited someone else’s pension
--WELSH You are retiring early due to poor health
--WELSH Your pension scheme allows early access (protected pension age)

radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether any of the options apply to you

options.text: --WELSH Sole trader (declare income through Self Assessment)
--WELSH Partner in a business partnership (declare income through Self Assessment)

radio-button.error: --WELSH Select what type of self-employed status you have.

eligibility-financial-settlement.title: --WELSH Has a financial settlement already been agreed?

sections.content: --WELSH You cannot book a divorce and separation appointment if a financial settlement has been agreed.

radio-button.label: --WELSH Has a financial settlement already been agreed?
radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether a financial settlement has already been agreed.

eligibility-pension-loss.title: --WELSH Have you lost pension money because of a scam or fraud?

sections.content: --WELSH This appointment is only for people who have been the victim of pension fraud.

radio-button.label: --WELSH Have you lost pension money because of a scam or fraud?
radio-button.error: --WELSH Please select whether you have lost pension money because of a scam or fraud.

pension-wise-not-eligible.title: --WELSH You’re not eligible for a Pension Wise appointment

sections.content: --WELSH You can’t book a Pension Wise appointment if:
items: --WELSH you’re under 50
--WELSH you only have a defined benefit pension (final salary or career-average salary) or
--WELSH you only have an overseas pension.

sections.content: --WELSH We can still help if you’re not eligible for Pension Wise. You can contact our pension specialists for free guidance:
items: --WELSH [use our webchat](#)
--WELSH [use our online form](#)

sections.content: --WELSH We’re open between 9am and 5pm, Monday to Friday. Closed on bank holidays.
--WELSH You can also use our [pension guides and tools.](#)

pension-loss-not-eligible.title: --WELSH You’re not eligible for a pension loss appointment

sections.content: --WELSH You can’t book a pension loss appointment if:
--WELSH We can still help if you’re not eligible for an appointment. You can contact our pension specialists for free guidance:

access-support.title: --WELSH Would you like any adjustments for your appointment?

sections.content: --WELSH A standard PensionWise appointment will be via telephone and last for approximately 1 hour. We can make adjustments to meet your needs. For example, you can ask for:
items: --WELSH A Welsh-speaking pension specialist
--WELSH A British Sign Language interpreter
--WELSH A foreign language interpreter
--WELSH Someone to attend the appointment with you
--WELSH Adjustments for accessibility needs

radio-button.error: --WELSH Select whether you would like adjustments for your appointment.

access-options.title: --WELSH What would help make your appointment easier?

options.text: --WELSH Someone to attend the appointment with you
options.hint: --WELSH We can use a conference call so they can join and provide support, even if they’re in a different location to you

radio-button.error: --WELSH Select whether you would like adjustments for your appointment.

text-area.label: --WELSH Anything else that would help? (Optional)
text-area.hint: --WELSH Tell us if there’s anything else that would make your appointment easier (for example, if you’re neurodivergent or prefer information explained slowly).

access-language.title: --WELSH What language do you need an interpreter for?

sections.content: --WELSH We can arrange an interpreter for most spoken languages. Choose your preferred language from the list below. If your language isn’t shown, select ‘Other language’ and tell us which one you need.

language-type.error: --WELSH Please select what language you need an interpreter for.

language-other.label: --WELSH Other language (please specify)
language-other.error: --WELSH Please specify which language you need an interpreter for.

help.title: --WELSH Need Help?
help.content:
--WELSH Speak to someone who can help book your free appointment on [0800 138 3944](tel:08001383944) or use our [webchat](https://webchat.moneyhelper.org.uk/newchat/chat.aspx?domain=www.moneyhelper.org.uk).

Call between 8am to 6:30pm, Monday to Friday.
